findings.title: Weak password hashing with MD5
findings.description: HashPassword uses md5.Sum, an unsalted and computationally cheap hash that is broken for password storage and trivially brute-forced or reversed via rainbow tables.
findings.suggestion: Replace md5.Sum with a memory-hard, salted password hashing function such as bcrypt (golang.org/x/crypto/bcrypt), scrypt, or Argon2id.
